claims.evidence_text: Ppd-D1a was the most potent known allele affecting the timing of flowering, requiring the least thermal time to flowering across all experiments... Ppd-D1a is the most potent photoperiod insensitive allele
claims.claim_summary: Photoperiod-insensitivity (Ppd-1a) alleles, especially Ppd-D1a, accelerate flowering and reduce thermal time to anthesis in wheat; Ppd-D1a is the most potent PI allele.

claims.evidence_text: with Ppd-D1a the most potent allele to induce flowering at the lowest mean thermal time of 1281 degree days (S.E.D. = 15.51)
claims.claim_summary: Most photoperiod-insensitive line (Ppd-D1a) reached male flowering at ~1281 degree-days from sowing; photoperiod-sensitive Ppd-1b control required ~1426 degree-days.

claims.evidence_text: Thermal time to flowering (deg C days): Ppd-1b 1426, Ppd-A1a 1293, Ppd-B1a 1348, Ppd-D1a 1281, Triple1 1327, Triple2 1351
claims.claim_summary: Thermal time from sowing to male flowering in wheat (cv. Paragon NILs) ranged 1281-1426 degree-days depending on photoperiod allele.

claims.evidence_text: Pots filled with the planting medium were soaked with water for 24 h prior to sowing with single seeds to a depth of 2.5 cm.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat seeds sown to 2.5 cm depth in pot-based experiment.

claims.evidence_text: Plots (minimum 2x 5 m) were drilled (300 seeds/m2) into a free-draining sandy loam
claims.claim_summary: Field wheat plots drilled at 300 seeds/m2.
